summary_one_line: A SELECT result-set containing a 'table.*' style wildcard reference (dot-star) that reaches the column-expansion true branch in selectExpander.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SELECT statement whose result columns include a qualified-wildcard reference (e.g. 'LLTL.*' — an identifier followed by '.' and '*'), so selectExpander reaches the true side of the wildcard/column-name expansion comparison at line 134117 rather than the degenerate false side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed is 'SELECT LLTL.*. .' — a SELECT with a qualified '.*' wildcard column reference; in the divergent-branch trace W takes the true side at the blocker (L134117 W: T=1 F=0) and progresses past it (W reaches L134120/L134132/L134154/L134252 with T=1), reflecting that the column-expansion path executed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are degenerate result lists like 'SELECT ( 111111,' and 'SELECT+SE+...' / 'SELECT ~~~~~44<4' with no '<ident>.*' wildcard token; in the trace L reaches L134112 (T=223) and L134114 but takes the false side at the blocker (L134117 L: T=0 F=223), never satisfying the comparison the true branch needs.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP records (PC, operand-distance-bucket) for the integer/string CMPs inside selectExpander's name-resolution / wildcard match; as havoc mutants drift their column-reference bytes toward a matching prefix, each closer operand pair opens a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that climbs toward the exact comparison the true branch gates on. naive's plain edge-counter feedback gets no such intermediate reward and only flips the branch by luck.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of a branch inside selectExpander, the SQL SELECT column-expansion routine.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
The winner-resolving seed is a SELECT containing a qualified wildcard reference 'LLTL.*'.
In the call-chain trace the winner takes the true side at the blocker while the loser takes the false side.
The loser reaches the lines just before the blocker (L134112 with T=223) but does not satisfy the true branch.
The naive loser seeds are degenerate result lists with repeated digits/operators and no qualified wildcard token.
value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket and rewards inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive provides only per-edge hit-count bucket feedback with no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is mandatory only for I2SRandReplace; the claimed mechanism here is the value_profile CMP_MAP distance-bucket gradient (winner = value_profile, loser = naive in the single decisive pair), so no I2S dash-row lineage check applies. The MECHANISM CONTEXT for value_profile explicitly states no I2SRandReplace and no dash rows are involved.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the true branch on a qualified-wildcard token where value_profile resolves no faster than naive (no dose-response in resolve-rate as the operand-match distance is widened), which would show CMP_MAP distance feedback gives no gradient at this comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner seed is available, so the inference that the '.*' qualified wildcard (rather than some other structural property of that single SELECT) is the gating feature rests on a single example.
